title: Joshua: Crossing into Promise
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of joshua: crossing into promise and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study joshua: crossing into promise and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What miracle marked Israel's crossing of the Jordan River into Canaan? Answer: the Jordan stopped flowing and stood as a heap while Israel crossed on dry ground, just as the Red Sea had parted for Moses.
segments.correct_answer: the Jordan stopped flowing and Israel crossed on dry ground
acceptable_variants: the waters of the jordan piled up and israel walked through on dry land
god stopped the jordan river so israel could cross
the Jordan parted as the Red Sea had parted for Moses

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The gold-covered box containing the tablets of the law; the priests carried it into the Jordan and its presence caused the river to stop, leading Israel's procession into the land
The mass circumcision of all males born in the wilderness in Joshua 5; the wilderness generation had not been circumcised, and God required the covenant sign before the first battle
